title: Live imaging of synaptic vesicle dynamics in human iPSC-derived Dopamine Neurons (DaNs) using synapto-pHluorin
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a443q","text":"This protocol utilizes synaptic vesicle-specific pH-sensitive synapto-pHluorin in a lentiviral vector to measure synaptic vesicle dynamics in iPSC-derived dopamine neurons following their differentiation using an expanded Krik’s protocol as described in Williamson, M.G. \u0026 Madureira, M. et al. (2023). This protocol employs electrical or chemical stimulation in concert with live imaging to measure various aspects of synaptic vesicle dynamics including vesicle pool size and recycling. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":254,"length":46}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4F93590A148B47588CA4C95A459B70E6","url":"https://doi.org/10.1093/hmg/ddad102"}}}}

steps: Thaw HEK293-T LentiX in high glucose 1x DMEM with Glutamax and 10% FBS.
Grow until ~90% confluence and then when ready, and prior to transfection, coat 15 cm dishes with poly-L-lysine for 1 hour at 37°C.
Transfect HEK cells with construct along with psPAX6, pMD2G and pAdVantage using Lipofectamine 3000. This should be done later in the day, and the next step done early the next morning.
The following morning, add ViralBoost as per manufacturer’s instructions along with fresh media.
Collect virus two to three days following transfection into a falcon tube and spin for 10 minutes at 500g.
Concentrate using Lenti-X concentrator as per manufacturer’s instructions for 48 hours at 4°C.
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1:100 (300 µL for 30 mL) of PBS.

Aliquot virus and store aliquots at -80°C. This can be done either simultaneous as replating or immediately following. Concentration of virus to be used is virus and batch-dependant and requires prior virus titration.
Plate coverslip into a pre-warmed bath of Classic Tyrodes Solution (see Materials ; 500 µL for a 18mm coverslip is sufficient ) on a suitable microscope.
Begin live imaging and allow for 30 seconds to 1 minute of recording to first occur to establish baseline fluorescence.

Provide electrical or chemical stimulation using 500 µL of High Stimulation Tyrodes Solution with high KCl (see Materials ). Prepare tyrodes buffer at 2x KCl concentration such that the classic tyrodes solution does not need to be first removed before adding on the additional solution. This allows for as little disruption to the cells being imaged as possible.
Obtain maximum signal by addition of Ammonium Tyrodes Solution (see Materials ).
Using ImageJ analyse individual region of interests (ROIs) along the neuronal processes determined based on the increase in fluorescence when ammonium was added (maximum signal).
Measure fluorescent intensity changes over time of >30 ROIs over time per recording.
creator: Cláudia C. Mendes
